pass1_value.note: Documentation specifies use of API key in Authorization header as a Bearer token and allows api_key query parameter for some endpoints.

final_value.note: The Authentication docs specify using an API key sent in the HTTP Authorization header as a Bearer token (i.e., `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`).

pass1_value.note: Help Center states API is available for Growth and higher plans, so a paid plan upgrade is required to obtain API access; generating the key is self-serve from the dashboard once on an eligible plan.

final_value.note: The help article states API access is only available on specific paid plans (e.g., Growth and above) and that eligible users can generate/manage the API key from their systeme.io account/dashboard (self-serve once on an eligible plan).

pass1_value.note: Developer portal lists REST API docs and multiple endpoints (Authentication, Endpoints, Webhooks, SDKs). The API covers core product objects (funnels, emails, contacts), per developer docs and portal.

final_value.note: The developer portal is a REST API documentation site with multiple resource endpoints and sections (including Authentication and Webhooks), indicating a broad REST surface across core systeme.io objects.

pass1_value.note: Public REST docs exist and API keys are generated from the dashboard, but API access requires upgrading to Growth or higher, so development is possible after routine paid plan upgrade.

final_value.note: Public REST documentation is available and authentication is via API key, but the official help article indicates API access requires being on an eligible paid plan (e.g., Growth+), making the primary blocker paid-only access.
